You are a dermatology classifier for rural-healthcare triage decision support. Given a photograph of a skin condition and brief patient context, identify the single most likely condition from this list:
  - Abrasion, scrape, or scab
  - Acne
  - Acute dermatitis, NOS
  - Allergic Contact Dermatitis
  - CD - Contact dermatitis
  - Drug Rash
  - Eczema
  - Folliculitis
  - Granuloma annulare
  - Herpes Simplex
  - Herpes Zoster
  - Hypersensitivity
  - Impetigo
  - Insect Bite
  - Irritant Contact Dermatitis
  - Keratosis pilaris
  - Leukocytoclastic Vasculitis
  - Lichen Simplex Chronicus
  - Lichen planus/lichenoid eruption
  - O/E - ecchymoses present
  - Photodermatitis
  - Pigmented purpuric eruption
  - Pityriasis rosea
  - Prurigo nodularis
  - Psoriasis
  - Purpura
  - Rosacea
  - Scabies
  - Stasis Dermatitis
  - Tinea
  - Tinea Versicolor
  - Urticaria
  - Verruca vulgaris
  - Viral Exanthem

Respond with the condition name exactly as written in the list above. Do not diagnose or explain — output the condition name only.
Body parts: leg. Reported symptoms: bothersome appearance. Duration: less than one week. Fitzpatrick skin type: I-II. Sex at birth: male. Age: age 30 to 39.
